title: Discovered: set_timeout in vault.py never persists timeout
design: src/lib/vault.py set_timeout(timeout_ms) assigns to a local _timeout that never escapes — FIXME left in code. Discovered while implementing Task 1 (wd1-4177092-t8l.1). Out of scope there. Fix: introduce module-level timeout state (or pass it through to the underlying client) and add a test that asserts the configured timeout is observable from a subsequent vault operation. Don't fix here — schedule separately.

title: Task 3: Synthetic-check hook for rotation window
design: Hook a synthetic check that runs every 30s during rotation window; assert zero 5xx. Files: src/lib/vault_rotate.py, tests/integration/test_rotate_synthetic.py.

title: Task 2: Add vault_rotate orchestration module
design: Create src/lib/vault_rotate.py with rotate(name, new_value) that calls vault.rotate_secret + schedules T+5min expiry. Files: src/lib/vault_rotate.py, tests/lib/test_vault_rotate.py.

title: Task 1: Add rotate_secret helper to vault.py
design: Extend src/lib/vault.py with rotate_secret(name, new_value) helper that stages a new versioned alias. Files: src/lib/vault.py, tests/lib/test_vault.py. Edge cases: concurrent calls serialize, vault unreachable raises VaultError, empty value rejected.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: rotate_secret(name, new_value) added to src/lib/vault.py with versioned-alias bookkeeping and per-name locking.

Evidence:
- SC1 (writes new versioned alias without touching old): src/lib/vault.py:37-38 (_stage_alias appends, never overwrites); test_rotate_stages_new_alias asserts both old and new versions are present.
- SC2 (secret returns new value after flip): src/lib/vault.py:48-52 (secret returns versions[-1]); test_secret_returns_new_after_flip.
- SC3 (5 unit tests pass write-staging / read-during-overlap / post-expiry): 5 new test functions in tests/lib/test_vault.py — test_rotate_stages_new_alias, test_secret_returns_new_after_flip, test_concurrent_rotate_serializes, test_vault_unreachable_raises, test_empty_value_rejected (None + ''). Post-expiry behavior is intentionally Task 2's scope (vault_rotate orchestration owns expiry timing); this task's overlap test asserts the prior version is still readable after staging.
- Edge case (concurrent serialize): _lock_for + per-name threading.Lock in rotate_secret; test_concurrent_rotate_serializes runs 4 parallel rotations and asserts the final version list has exactly 5 entries (1 base + 4 staged).
- Edge case (vault unreachable, no partial-write): try/except wraps _stage_alias raises into VaultError; test_vault_unreachable_raises asserts only the base alias remains after a failing stage.
- Edge case (empty/None rejected): explicit guard at the top of rotate_secret; test_empty_value_rejected parametrized over None and ''.
- Tests: pytest -v -> 7 passed in 0.05s (1 baseline smoke + 6 new test cases from 5 functions).

Deviations:
- Spec convention says 'exceptions inherit acme.errors.VaultError', but no acme.errors module exists in the sandbox. Task 1 files_touched is scoped to vault.py + test_vault.py, so creating acme.errors would expand scope. Used the existing vault.VaultError class. Flagged earlier in this notes thread.

Discoveries filed:
- wd1-4177092-pdh: set_timeout in vault.py never persists timeout (FIXME left in source, out of Task 1 scope). Linked via discovered-from dependency.

Status: in_progress (leaving for reviewer per work-do skill — close transition is the reviewer's via work-audit).
